title: Skill Contract
description: Machine-readable contracts for the skills in 04-skills.md. The runtime uses these to validate inputs and route to the correct capability. See spec/BRAIN_SPEC.md §04-skills.md for requirements.

service.description: The service this contract set covers. Must match 01-service-definition.md.

version.description: Contract version. Increment when any skill contract changes.

contracts.description: One entry per skill. Each skill must correspond to a named skill in 04-skills.md.

name.description: Skill name in snake_case. Must match the skill name in 04-skills.md exactly.

description.description: One sentence. What the skill does, from the perspective of the workflow.

trigger.description: What causes this skill to be invoked. Can be an event name, a step in the workflow, or a condition.

inputs.description: Input parameters. Each key is a parameter name; each value describes it.

type.description: Data type. Examples: string, integer, boolean, array, object, date.

required.description: Whether this input is required for the skill to run.

outputs.description: Output fields. Each key is a field name; each value describes it.

capabilities.description: Abstract capability names this skill uses. The runtime maps these to real tools via the adapter layer. Examples: send_message, lookup_record, write_file, read_spreadsheet.

approval_required.description: Whether a human must approve before this skill executes. Must be consistent with 05-guardrails.md.

approval_format.description: If approval_required is true: what the approval request must include. Example: 'Slack DM to @ops-lead with draft output attached'.

notes.description: Anything a developer needs to know when implementing this skill.
